name: Chest Lift
setup: Lying supine, legs bent, shins perpendicular to floor, feet hip width apart, fingers interlaced behind head

movement.action: Lift head and chest
Pause
Lower chest and head, return to start position
Pause

cues: Keep elbows wide
Keep legs parallel
Support head with hands
Keep lumbar spine in contact with barrel

name: Reach
setup: Sitting in C curve position, arms straight, shoulder width apart, shoulder height, hands holding pole, legs bent and together, shins perpendicular position to floor

movement.action: Roll down, lift arms up and overhead, simultaneously straighten legs creating straight line
Pause
Roll up, lift arms up and forward, bend legs, return to start position
Pause

cues: Establish C curve position, shoulders directly above hips
Establish straight line with body when in supine position
Move trunk, arms and legs simultaneously when rolling down to supine position and rolling up to sitting position

name: Overhead Stretch
setup: Sitting upright, arms straight, shoulder width apart, shoulder height, palms facing each other, legs bent, shins perpendicular to floor, feet hip width apart

movement.action: Roll down, lift arms up and overhead
Circle arms around lifting head
Roll up to C curve position
Extend trunk, sit upright, return to start position

cues: Keep head aligned with spine
Lift arms overhead when trunk reaches full extension
Coordinate lifting head as arms circle around
Rest back and head on barrel when arms are overhead

name: Teaser Prep
setup: Lying supine, coccyx on edge of step, arms straight and overhead, palms facing each other, legs straight and together, feet plantarflexed, body on diagonal line

movement.action: Lift arms, head and chest up and forward, simultaneously lift legs to perpendicular position
Lower arms, head, chest and legs, return to start position

cues: Lift legs perpendicular to floor, arms parallel to floor
Engage abdominals prior to lifting legs
Bring trunk close to legs to form pike position
Allow lower back to sink into step of barrel

name: Openings
setup: Lying supine, lower back and pelvis on barrel, legs straight, perpendicular to floor, hips externally rotated, feet plantarflexed, hands holding handles

movement.action: Open legs out to sides
Close legs, return to start position

cues: Maintain pelvic lumbar stabilization
Open legs directly out to sides
Pause in perpendicular position between each repetition
Keep holding handles firmly and pulling barrel toward back

name: Helicopter
setup: Starting in Openings position (page 55), legs externally rotated

movement.action: Open legs to Scissors position
Circle legs around to Scissors position on opposite side, return to start position

cues: Maintain pelvic stabilization
Touch each point of circle as legs circle around
Pass through perpendicular position following each circle
Keep feet at consistent distance from floor throughout circle
Keep holding handles firmly and pulling barrel toward back

name: Scissors
setup: Starting in Openings position (page 55), legs parallel

movement.action: Open legs to Scissors position (2 pulses)
Switch legs
Open legs to Scissors position (2 pulses)
Switch legs

cues: Maintain pelvic lumbar stabilization
Create even V in Scissors position
Pass through perpendicular position following each circle
Keep holding handles firmly and pulling barrel toward back

name: Bicycle
setup: Starting in Scissors position (page 57), open legs to V position

movement.action: Bend back knee, touch step with toes, move knee toward chest, straighten leg over face, simultaneously move opposite leg up and back to create Scissors position on opposite side
Repeat above sequence

cues: Maintain pelvic lumbar stabilization
Create V shape in Scissors position
Avoid externally rotating hip when bending back knee

name: Bicycle Reverse
setup: Starting in Scissors position (page 57), open legs to V position

movement.action: Bend front knee toward chest, move leg back, touch step with toes, straighten leg, simultaneously move opposite leg up and forward to create Scissors position on opposite side
Repeat above sequence

cues: Maintain pelvic lumbar stabilization
Maximize Scissors position prior to bending knee
Avoid externally rotating hip when bending back knee

name: Roll Over
setup: Lying supine with lower back and pelvis on barrel, legs straight and together, feet plantarflexed, hands holding handles, body on diagonal line

movement.action: Lift legs to 90 degrees in hip joint
Roll over, legs parallel to floor
Dorsiflex feet, separate legs, lower legs toward mat
Roll back, lift legs perpendicular to floor, plantarflex feet, lower legs to diagonal line, bring legs together, return to start position

cues: Maintain 90 degree angle in hip joint during roll over
Keep thighs close to chest when rolling back
Avoid lowering legs beyond diagonal line

name: Shoulder Stretch Lying Side
setup: Lying sideways on barrel, bottom leg bent, top leg straight, feet plantarflexed, arms straight overhead, head resting on bottom arm

movement.action: Circle top arm around to side, simultaneously roll body into supine position, externally rotating hips
Continue circling arm to side of body, simultaneously return to side lying position, circle arm forward and overhead, return to start position

cues: Coordinate movement of arm with movement of body
Keep neck relaxed and allow head to rest on arm and barrel

name: Spine Twist Supine
setup: Lying supine with lower back and pelvis on barrel, legs in tabletop position, thighs perpendicular to floor, hands holding handles

movement.action: Lower legs to one side
Lift legs to center, return to start position
Lower legs to one side
Lift legs to center, return to start position

cues: Maintain pelvic lumbar stabilization
Initiate rotation to side and back to center with abdominals
Move legs and pelvis as one unit
Keep holding handles firmly and pulling barrel toward back

name: Side Lift
setup: Lying sideways on barrel, bottom leg bent, top leg straight, feet plantarflexed, arms straight overhead, head between arms, leg, trunk and arms in straight line

movement.action: Lift trunk
Lower trunk, return to start position

objectives: Spinal lateral flexor strength and stretch
cues: Keep head aligned with spine and between arms
Maintain co-contraction of abdominals and back extensors
Imagine moving between two panes of glass
Keep arms straight

name: Corkscrew
setup: Starting in Scissors position (page 57)

movement.action: Lower legs to one side
Circle legs down and around to opposite side, return to start position

cues: Keep legs together
Move legs and pelvis as one unit
Avoid lowering legs beyond diagonal line
Initiate rotation to side and back to center with abdominals
Keep holding handles firmly and pulling barrel toward back

name: Corkscrew Advanced
setup: Starting in Roll Over position (page 60), legs overhead

movement.action: Rotate spine, moving legs to one side
Roll down side of trunk, circle legs around, rotate spine, roll up opposite side of trunk, rotate spine, moving legs to center, return to start position

cues: Keep legs together
Move legs and pelvis as one unit
Avoid lowering legs beyond diagonal line
Initiate rotation to side and back to center with abdominals
Maintain connection with barrel

name: Swan Prep
setup: Lying prone on barrel, legs slightly bent, hip width apart and parallel, feet dorsiflexed, toes anchored on floor, hands behind head

movement.action: Lift trunk to diagonal line
Straighten arms overhead
Bend arms, placing hands behind head
Lower trunk, return to start position

cues: Keep head aligned with spine
Maximize spinal articulation in both directions
Maintain abdominal engagement to protect lower back
Create V position with arms when reaching overhead
Touch fingers loosely behind head

name: Swan
setup: Starting in Swan Prep position (page 66), hips externally rotated, feet in V position

movement.action: Lift trunk
Straighten arms overhead, simultaneously straighten legs, plantarflex feet
Bend arms, placing hands behind head
Lower trunk and legs, dorsiflex feet, anchor toes on floor, return to start position

cues: Keep head aligned with spine
Maximize spinal articulation in both directions
Maintain abdominal engagement to protect lower back
Create V position with arms when reaching overhead

name: Reach (F2 Springs)
setup: Sitting in C curve position, arms straight, shoulder width apart, shoulder height, hands holding handles, legs bent and together, shins perpendicular position to floor
resistance: Socket C, Lever Orientation 0, Lever Length 1, Lever Direction 3, Connector O, Spring Y/S

movement.action: Roll down, lift arms up and overhead, simultaneously straighten legs creating straight line, continue reaching arms overhead, placing head on barrel
Pause
Lift arms and head to straight line, roll up, continue lifting arms up and forward, bend legs, return to start position
Pause

cues: Establish straight line with body prior to and following placing head on barrel, arms overhead

name: Hundred Prep (F2 Springs)
setup: Lying supine over barrel, arms straight, reaching overhead, hands holding handles, legs bent and together, shins perpendicular to floor
resistance: Socket C, Lever Orientation 0, Lever Length 1, Lever Direction 7, Connector O, Spring Y/S

movement.action: Lift arms, head and chest up and forward
Pause
Lower chest and head, reaching arms up and overhead, return to start position
Pause

cues: Keep legs together
Maintain contact of lumbar spine with barrel
Continue reaching arms overhead to maximum range of shoulders, maintaining tension in springs

name: Teaser Prep (F2 Springs)
setup: Lying supine over barrel, arms straight, reaching overhead, hands holding handles, legs straight and together, feet plantarflexed, legs aligned with trunk
resistance: Socket C, Lever Orientation 0, Lever Length 1, Lever Direction 7, Connector O, Spring Y/S

movement.action: Lift arms, head and chest up and forward, arms parallel to floor, simultaneously lift legs perpendicular to floor (as close as possible)
Pause
Lower chest and head, reaching arms up and overhead, simultaneously lower legs, return to start position
Pause

cues: Keep legs together
Continue reaching arms overhead to maximum range of shoulders, maintaining tension in springs

name: Frog (F2 Springs)
setup: Lying supine, lower back and pelvis on barrel, shoulders on mat, hands holding levers, legs bent, hips externally rotated, feet dorsiflexed in straps, heels together
resistance: Socket C, Lever Orientation 0, Lever Length 1, Lever Direction 5, Connector O, Spring Y/S

movement.action: Straighten legs up
Bend legs, return to start position

cues: Avoid bending knees beyond 90 degrees
Initiate movement with hip adductors
Establish perpendicular position with legs when straight
Keep holding handles firmly and pulling barrel toward back

name: Openings (F2 Springs)
setup: Starting in Frog position (page 72), hips externally rotated, legs straight and together, perpendicular to floor, feet plantarflexed
resistance: Socket C, Lever Orientation 0, Lever Length 1, Lever Direction 5, Connector O, Spring Y/S

movement.action: Open legs out to sides
Close legs, return to start position

cues: Maintain pelvic lumbar stabilization
Open legs as wide as possible while maintaining spring tension
Pause in perpendicular position between each repetition
Keep holding handles firmly and pulling barrel toward back

name: Scissors (F2 Springs)
setup: Starting in Frog position (page 72), hips externally rotated, legs straight and together, perpendicular to floor, feet plantarflexed
resistance: Socket C, Lever Orientation 0, Lever Length 1, Lever Direction 5, Connector O, Spring Y/S

movement.action: Open legs to Scissors position (2 pulses)
Switch legs

cues: Maintain pelvic lumbar stabilization
Create even V in Scissors position
Pass through the perpendicular position following each repetition
Keep holding handles firmly and pulling barrel toward back

name: Helicopter (F2 Springs)
setup: Starting in Frog position (page 72), hips externally rotated, legs straight and together, perpendicular to floor, feet plantarflexed
resistance: Socket C, Lever Orientation 0, Lever Length 1, Lever Direction 5, Connector O, Spring Y/S

movement.action: Open legs to Scissors position (page 74)
Circle legs around to Scissors position on opposite side, return to start position

cues: Touch each point of the circle as legs circle around
Pass through the perpendicular position following each circle
Keep feet at consistent distance from mat throughout circle

name: Roll Over (F2 Springs)
setup: Lying supine with lower back and pelvis on barrel, legs straight and together, feet plantarflexed, hands holding levers, body on diagonal line
resistance: Socket C, Lever Orientation 0, Lever Length 1, Lever Direction 5, Connector O, Spring Y/S

movement.action: Lift legs to 90 degrees in hip joint
Roll over, legs parallel to floor
Dorsiflex feet, separate legs, lower legs toward mat
Roll back, lift legs perpendicular to floor, plantarflex feet, lower legs to diagonal line, bring legs together, return to start position

objectives: Lower back and hip extensor stretch
cues: Maintain 90 degree angle in hip joint during roll over
Keep thighs close to chest when rolling back
Avoid lowering legs beyond diagonal line

name: Side Lift (F2 Springs)
setup: Lying sideways on barrel, bottom leg bent, foot plantarflexed, top leg straight, foot plantarflexed in strap, arms straight and overhead
resistance: Socket C, Lever Orientation 0, Lever Length 1, Lever Direction 7, Connector O, Spring Y/S

movement.action: Lift trunk
Lower trunk

objectives: Spinal lateral flexor strength and stretch
cues: Keep head aligned with spine and between arms
Maintain co-contraction of abdominals and back extensors
Imagine moving between two panes of glass
Keep arms straight
Keep leg straight, maintaining tension in spring

name: Swan (F2 Springs)
setup: Lying prone over barrel, feet on floor, legs bent and together, hips externally rotated, feet in V position, hands on levers, elbows out to sides
resistance: Socket C, Lever Orientation 0, Lever Length 1, Lever Direction 7, Connector C, Spring N/A

movement.action: Lift trunk, straightening arms and legs, plantarflex feet
Lower trunk, bending arms and legs, place feet on floor, return to position

cues: Maintain abdominal support
Avoid scapular elevation as arms straighten
Keep legs straight and hips extended as back lifts
Maintain contact between pelvis and barrel
